what_it_does: Records a go/no-go compatibility check and a reuse inventory for enabling two-way editing/sync between an Obsidian vault and a typed ledger of markdown items.
observations: Frontmatter declares id, board:// address, type:block, source:claude_code, state:current, title 'OB4 · The decisive check + what to reuse', and a part_of link to board://item-156ec226.
The body makes a single gating question: whether Obsidian preserves nested 'links:'/'history:' YAML arrays and closed key order on a round-trip.
If Obsidian mangles the YAML, two-way sync is declared off and write-back must route through a custom serializer, never Obsidian touching canonical files.

It lists reusable existing pieces: ~/company vault conventions, a Status Board.base over type:issue notes, substrate-mcp reconciling typed-graph + markdown + wikilinks, and a canonical Obsidian Builder vault with 17 plugins plus custom plugins.
It lists three gaps: no ~/company/.obsidian/ directory yet, two address-graph engines still need converging, and typed-address edges are not native wikilinks.
standouts: The round-trip YAML fidelity check is framed as the single gate for all two-way behavior.
The work is framed as completing an existing direction rather than greenfield building.
substrate-mcp is identified as the likely convergence point for two address-graph engines.
conventions: YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, links, and history fields.
Markdown body with an H1 and bold emphasis for key assertions.
Noticeboard item format with a part_of link to a parent board item.
Use of board:// URI scheme for internal item addresses.
concerns: The file asserts Obsidian's behavior without providing observed evidence.
The 'closed key order' requirement is strict and may conflict with normal YAML parser behavior.
The two-way plan depends on resolving several unverified gaps in the same step.
notes: This item sets the make-or-break Obsidian YAML round-trip test and maps what can be reused versus what still needs bridging.
questions: What exact YAML serialization rules define 'closed key order' in this context?
Which Obsidian workflow or plugin performs the canonical round-trip test?
How does substrate-mcp reconcile typed-graph addresses with markdown/wikilinks in practice?
What are the two address-graph engines and how do they differ?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to define the decisive pre-build check and what to reuse; the file does exactly that.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a hard go/no-go gate for Obsidian-native editing of ledger files and to steer implementation toward existing substrates instead of building new tooling from scratch.
novelty: It treats Obsidian's YAML round-trip fidelity as the single decisive gate for bidirectional editing, and positions substrate-mcp as the convergence point for two separate address-graph engines.
contribution: Records the round-trip fidelity gate, enumerates reusable existing components, and identifies three concrete gaps (.obsidian absence, address-graph engine convergence, typed-address edges vs wikilinks) that a fused system would need to resolve.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item OB4 states a gating Obsidian YAML round-trip fidelity check for two-way ledger sync and inventories reusable vault components and remaining gaps.
intention_for_embedding: To decide whether Obsidian can safely edit canonical ledger files and to guide implementation toward reusing existing substrates.
